message:
feat(engine): add Float64 type with IEEE 754 semantics, upgrade hash to SHA-256, fix IR determinism, and apply rustfmt cleanup

Core feature additions:
- Introduce Float64 wrapper around f64 providing deterministic total ordering
  (via total_cmp), Eq/Ord/Hash impls, and JavaScript-compatible Display
  (NaN, Infinity, -Infinity, -0 handling)
- Add Value::Float(Float64) variant to the runtime value representation with
  proper truthiness (NaN and 0.0/-0.0 are falsy), typeof ("number"),
  coerce_to_float() for arithmetic, and abstract_eq_values() with NaN !== NaN
  and -0 == +0 semantics
- Add heap budget and memory budget unit tests for interpreter config
- Add 10 regex literal parsing unit tests (simple patterns, flags, escapes,
  character classes, complex patterns, error cases, hash stability)

Security hardening:
- Replace hand-rolled SipHash-inspired Merkle-Damgard collision_resistant_hash
  with actual SHA-256 via the sha2 crate; retain SipInspiredCr enum variant
  name for serialization compatibility; add SHA-256 known-vector test cases

Determinism fix:
- ScopeNode::canonical_value() now sorts bindings by binding_id with
  multi-field tiebreakers (name, scope_depth, scope_index, kind) before
  serialization, ensuring stable hashes regardless of insertion order
- Add 5 tests verifying ordering stability and tiebreak behavior

Style cleanup (rustfmt pass):
- Collapse unnecessarily split serde_json::to_string/from_str chains across
  ~50 source files
- Replace checked_add(1).unwrap_or(MAX) with saturating_add(1)
- Remove redundant lifetime annotations
- Normalize import ordering
- Reformat HostCall destructure patterns for consistency

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -805,8 +805,7 @@ fn verify_attestation_layer(receipt: &OptReceipt, input: &AttestationLayerInput)
             "attestation_policy_measurement_mismatch",
             format!(
                 "policy measurement ({}:{}) != expected (sha256:{})",
-                serde_json::to_string(&input.policy_quote.measurement.algorithm)
-                    .unwrap(),
+                serde_json::to_string(&input.policy_quote.measurement.algorithm).unwrap(),
                 input.policy_quote.measurement.digest_hex,
                 expected_measurement_digest
             ),
